yesterdayAnalysis: Saturday's long ride delivered exactly what the transition week called for: 55.7mi on the ROUVY Oregon course preview at 114 TSS, keeping intensity controlled (HR 138). The 1-mile brick run at HR 175 was brief but completed the key back-loaded session. This was the week's anchor workout and it hit all targets perfectly for race-specific preparation.
todayRecommendation: Complete today's easy 3-4mi run at pure aerobic effort to close out the transition week. Given last night's poor sleep (score 49, elevated RHR 59) and the week's volume already at 117% of target, keep this truly easy and hip-protective — no intensity whatsoever.
weekProgress: Week 9 of 10 is complete at 374 of 320 TSS (117%), perfectly executing the transition week taper strategy. All prescribed sessions were completed: both brick workouts (Mon/Wed/Sat), both quality swims (Thu pool with race pace, Fri open water), and the critical 50-55mi Saturday ride. The -35% volume reduction from build weeks was implemented as planned while maintaining sharpness.
keyInsight: Two consecutive nights of poor sleep (scores 48 and 49) following the Saturday long ride suggest incomplete recovery — today's easy run must remain strictly aerobic to avoid digging a hole before next week's final Oregon 70.3 tune-up.
